source_pr.title: executor: deep copy json into partial result
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

expression.EvalJSON() only returns a shallow copy of the JSON content in Chunk. We need to deep copy it to avoid dirty memory.

### What is changed and how it works?

deep copy JSON into partial result

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch
 - Need to be included in the release note

symptoms: tk.MustQuery(`select b from t group by a order by a;`).Check(testkit.Rows( (executor/aggregate_test.go)
failure_signature: tk.MustQuery(`select b from t group by a order by a;`).Check(testkit.Rows( (executor/aggregate_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: JSON values returned from chunk evaluation were only shallow-copied, so later reuse/mutation of the underlying buffer could change previously stored partial results ("dirty" memory/aliasing). The fix deep-copies JSON values before storing them in partial results.
fix_pattern: Deep-copy JSON values (Copy()) before persisting into partial results to avoid unsafe aliasing/mutable backing buffers.

evidence: evidence: tk.MustQuery(`select b from t group by a order by a;`).Check(testkit.Rows( (executor/aggregate_test.go)
